formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Vant Hoff Factor Law",
    "formula": "\\chi = \\frac{\\text{Number of particles in solution}}{\\text{Number of formula units in solution}}",
    "variables": "χ (Vant Hoff Factor), Number of particles in solution, Number of formula units in solution",
    "units": "dimensionless",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the Vant Hoff factor for solutions of electrolytes"
  },
  {
    "name": "Raoult's Law",
    "formula": "\\Delta P = P_0 \\chi X",
    "variables": "\\Delta P (change in vapor pressure), P_0 (vapor pressure of pure solvent), \\chi (Vant Hoff factor), X (mole fraction of solute)",
    "units": "\\Delta P (Pa), P_0 (Pa), \\chi (dimensionless), X (dimensionless)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the change in vapor pressure of a solution"
  },
  {
    "name": "Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures",
    "formula": "P_A = P_{ATM} \\times \\frac{n_A}{n_{ATM}}",
    "variables": "P_A (partial pressure of gas A), P_{ATM} (atmospheric pressure), n_A (number of moles of gas A), n_{ATM} (number of moles of atmospheric gases)",
    "units": "P_A (Pa), P_{ATM} (Pa), n_A (mol), n_{ATM} (mol)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the partial pressure of a gas in a mixture"
  },
  {
    "name": "Henderson-Hasselbalch Equation",
    "formula": "pH = pKa + log\\left(\\frac{[A^-]}{[HA]}\\right)",
    "variables": "pH (acidity), pKa (acid dissociation constant), [A^-] (concentration of conjugate base), [HA] (concentration of acid)",
    "units": "pH (dimensionless), pKa (dimensionless), [A^-] (M), [HA] (M)",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the pH of a buffer solution"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Independent Assortment",
    "formula": "No mathematical expression",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "States that alleles for different genes are sorted independently of each other during meiosis"
  },
  {
    "name": "Mendel's Law of Segregation",
    "formula": "No mathematical expression",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "States that each pair of alleles separates during gamete formation"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Dominance",
    "formula": "No mathematical expression",
    "variables": "None",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "States that one allele will be expressed over the other allele in a heterozygous individual"
  },
  {
    "name": "Weber's Law",
    "formula": "\\Delta J = KJ \\frac{\\Delta L}{L}",
    "variables": "\\Delta J (just-noticeable difference), KJ (Weber's constant), \\Delta L (change in stimulus intensity), L (stimulus intensity)",
    "units": "\\Delta J (dimensionless), KJ (dimensionless), \\Delta L (dimensionless), L (dimensionless)",
    "notes": "Used to describe the relationship between stimulus intensity and the just-noticeable difference"
  }
]
```

Note: The above JSON array includes various formulas related to plant biology, including those from the Morphology of Flowering Plants chapter.
